ref: The servants came and asked, “Sir, didn't you scatter good seed in your field? Where did these weeds come from?”
text: Sɔŋɔ tu wʋm tɩntʋŋna laan ma vu ba ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: ‹Dɩbam yuutu, nmʋ na dugi n kara kam tɩn, kʋ daɩ mɩn-ŋʋna yɩranɩ mʋ n dugi na? Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ mɩn-kayalɩ laan nuŋi sɩ gwaanɩ da?›
translated: Translation: "Since we were talking about the topic of learning a new language, do you understand a little bit of it, or do you find it difficult, and why do you think it's hard for you to learn a new language, or are you saying that it's easy for you?"

ref: Two men will be in the same field, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
text: Kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ, nɔɔna bale na wʋ kara nɩ ba tʋŋa, Wɛ wʋ́ lɩ ba dɩdʋa DƖ daarɩ wʋdoŋ wʋm.
translated: The story begins when a man named Kam, who was very skilled at carving, was invited by a group to join them. We were living in a place called DƖ, and we had a tradition of carving wooden statues.

ref: Jesus replied, “Moses gave you this law because you are so heartless.
text: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Moyisi dɛɛn pʋpʋnɩ kʋntʋ o pa abam, á wʋrʋ na dana tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ.
translated: Zezi said to him: "Moyisi does not want to play with the boys, but he likes to dance with the girls."

ref: You blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of a cup, and then the outside will also be clean.
text: Farizɩan tu, nmʋ nyɩ dɩ lilwe mʋ te. Nmʋ maŋɩ sɩ n da yigǝ n sɩn n zwɩ wʋ mʋ, sɩ sɩ kwɛ dɩ daa yɩ taa jɩgɩ digiru.
translated: Fariz, you are not the only one who is tired. We may not have the same resources as some, but we can still work together to dig out the ditch.

ref: To swear by heaven is the same as swearing by God's throne and by the one who sits on that throne.
text: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ dɩ nan na dugi dɩ Wɛ-sɔŋɔ kʋm tɩn, kʋ brɩ nɩ kʋntʋ tu dugi dɩ Wɛ paarɩ jangɔŋɔ kʋm mʋ, kʋ brɩ nɩ kʋntʋ tu dugi dɩ Baŋa-Wɛ mʋ.
translated: The story takes place in the land of the Wɛ-songo people, where there was a man who had two wives, a woman from the Wɛ people and another from the Baŋa-Wɛ tribe.

ref: While the Pharisees were still there, Jesus asked them,
text: Farizɩan tiinǝ badonnǝ dɛɛn ma ba ba kikili daanɩ. Zezi ma zaŋɩ o bwe-ba o wɩ:
translated: "Farizhan, you don't have any money to buy a car."

ref: But the girl's mother told her to say, “Here on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
text: Bukɔ wʋm nu ma ta o brɩ-o, o na wʋ́ loori kʋlʋ tɩn. O laan ma vu Erɔdɩ te o wɩ: «Woŋo kʋlʋ a na lagɩ tɩn, kʋ yɩ Zan yuu mʋ. Nmʋ na goni-kʋ, sɩ n kɩ zʋŋ-tanlaŋa wʋnɩ n pa-nɩ.»
translated: The book we were discussing earlier, it seems, has been stolen. A man named Erodan said: "Someone has taken it, I think it's Zan. He was here, and now it's missing from its place. I suggest we search everywhere and question him."

ref: Finally, about five in the afternoon the man went back and found some others standing there. He asked them, “Why have you been standing here all day long doing nothing?”
text: Dɩdaan-ni nɩ o daa ma vu yaga o na nɔɔna badonnǝ ta na zɩgɩ da. O ma bwe-ba o wɩ: ‹Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ abam zɩgɩ yo seeni dɛ dɩm wʋnɩ maama, yɩ á ba kɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ?›
translated: The man said to the woman: "Why did you go to the market alone today? I heard that a stranger was there, did he bother you?"

ref: While Jesus was still speaking, some people came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
text: Zezi ta na wʋra o ŋɔɔnɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, nɔɔna badonnǝ ma nuŋi Zayirusi sɔŋɔ ba ba, ba ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «N bukɔ wʋm tɩga. Daa n yɩ ta n jɩgɩ karanyɩna kam n yaara.»
translated: Zezi said to the group of people gathered around the fire, "But we must be careful. There is a dangerous creature lurking in the forest. It has three eyes and a long, sharp tail."

ref: The chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses heard what Jesus said, and they started looking for a way to kill him. They were afraid of him, because the crowds were completely amazed at his teaching.
text: Zwifǝ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ ba cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam ma ni Zezi na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn. Ba laan ma beeri cwǝŋǝ sɩ ba gʋ-o. Ba ma kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ dɩd-o, o zaasɩm dɩm na sʋ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm maama tɩn ŋwaanɩ.
translated: Zwifka cannot enter the room because all the cars in the room are parked closely together. But if there is a space, it can squeeze through a small gap in the wall and enter the room easily.
